items.title: Build a lightweight threat model before reading code
items.description: Identify assets, trust boundaries, and the most likely attackers so review time targets the riskiest code paths first.
items.how_to_find: Review Methodology — Review Approach: Review Methodology — Review Approach: Review Methodology — Review Approach: Review Methodology — Review Approach: Confirm a threat model or data-flow diagram exists; map authentication boundaries, privilege levels, and where untrusted data enters the system
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for build a lightweight threat model before reading code, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Map all entry points (attack surface)
items.description: Enumerate every place untrusted data enters: HTTP routes, message queues, CLI args, file imports, deserializers, webhooks, and IPC.
items.how_to_find: Find route definitions, controller mappings, event handlers, and parsers; ensure each entry point has identified input validation and authorization.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for map all entry points (attack surface), apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

references.title: OWASP Attack Surface Analysis Cheat Sheet

items.title: Perform source-to-sink taint analysis
items.description: Trace tainted (user-controlled) data from sources to security-sensitive sinks, noting any sanitizers or encoders along the path.
items.how_to_find: For each sink (query, exec, file open, template render), trace backward to its source; flag any path where tainted data reaches the sink without sanitization.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for perform source-to-sink taint analysis, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Inspect trust boundary crossings
items.description: Wherever data crosses a trust boundary (client to server, service to service, tenant to tenant) validation and authorization must be re-applied server-side.
items.how_to_find: Review Methodology — Review Approach: Review Methodology — Review Approach: Review Methodology — Review Approach: Review Methodology — Review Approach: Confirm validation is not delegated to the client; check that server-side code re-validates and re-authorizes data received from less-trusted layers
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for inspect trust boundary crossings, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Prioritize security-relevant diffs in PR reviews
items.description: In incremental reviews, focus on changes touching auth, crypto, parsers, queries, and dependency updates rather than reviewing line-by-line uniformly.
items.how_to_find: Review Methodology — Review Approach: Review Methodology — Review Approach: Review Methodology — Review Approach: Review Methodology — Review Approach: Confirm the diff is reviewed for risky changes: new sinks, weakened checks, removed validation, or added dependencies
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for prioritize security-relevant diffs in pr reviews, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Configure SAST with project-appropriate rulesets
items.description: Stand up a SAST engine (Semgrep, CodeQL, SonarQube) tuned to the languages and frameworks in use, and run it in CI on every change.
items.how_to_find: Review Methodology — Tooling & Automation: Review Methodology — Tooling & Automation: Review Methodology — Tooling & Automation: Review Methodology — Tooling & Automation: Confirm SAST runs in CI, uses curated rule packs for the stack, and that high-severity findings block merges or require sign-off
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for configure sast with project-appropriate rulesets, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Grep for dangerous sink patterns
items.description: Use ripgrep to quickly locate high-risk APIs: exec/system, raw SQL, eval, deserializers, innerHTML, and disabled TLS verification.
items.how_to_find: Review Methodology — Tooling & Automation: Review Methodology — Tooling & Automation: Review Methodology — Tooling & Automation: Review Methodology — Tooling & Automation: Run targeted patterns (e.g
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for grep for dangerous sink patterns, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Write custom rules for project-specific sinks
items.description: Encode internal dangerous helpers (custom query builders, in-house exec wrappers) as Semgrep/CodeQL rules so they are caught automatically.
items.how_to_find: Review Methodology — Tooling & Automation: Review Methodology — Tooling & Automation: Review Methodology — Tooling & Automation: Review Methodology — Tooling & Automation: Confirm custom Semgrep patterns or CodeQL queries exist for project-specific sink functions and unsafe internal wrappers
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for write custom rules for project-specific sinks, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Triage SAST findings and suppress with justification
items.description: Investigate findings for exploitability and false positives; suppress only with a documented reason, never blanket-disable rules.
items.how_to_find: Review Methodology — Tooling & Automation: Review Methodology — Tooling & Automation: Review Methodology — Tooling & Automation: Review Methodology — Tooling & Automation: Check that suppressions (nosem, // codeql, sonar:ignore) carry a justification and are reviewed, and that suppression counts are not growing unchecked
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for triage sast findings and suppress with justification, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Diff against history to spot weakened controls
items.description: Compare versions to detect removed validation, downgraded crypto, new debug flags, or silently added dependencies.
items.how_to_find: Review Methodology — Tooling & Automation: Review Methodology — Tooling & Automation: Review Methodology — Tooling & Automation: Review Methodology — Tooling & Automation: Use git diff/blame across the change range to confirm no security control was removed or weakened and that risky additions are intentional
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for diff against history to spot weakened controls, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Detect string-concatenated SQL queries
items.description: Queries assembled by concatenating or interpolating user input into the SQL string are classic SQLi sinks.
items.how_to_find: Look for SQL strings built with +, string interpolation, sprintf/format, or template literals containing request data instead of bound parameters.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for detect string-concatenated sql queries, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

references.title: OWASP SQL Injection Prevention Cheat Sheet

items.title: Review ORM raw query and rawSQL escape hatches
items.description: ORMs are safe by default but expose raw query methods (e.g.
items.how_to_find: Injection Sinks — SQL Injection Sinks: Injection Sinks — SQL Injection Sinks: Injection Sinks — SQL Injection Sinks: Injection Sinks — SQL Injection Sinks: Search for ORM raw query APIs and confirm any user input is passed as parameters, not concatenated into the raw string
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for review orm raw query and rawsql escape hatches, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

references.title: OWASP SQL Injection Prevention Cheat Sheet

items.title: Check dynamic table/column identifiers and ORDER BY
items.description: Parameterization does not cover identifiers (table/column names, ORDER BY, LIMIT); these must be allowlisted, not bound.
items.how_to_find: Injection Sinks — SQL Injection Sinks: Injection Sinks — SQL Injection Sinks: Injection Sinks — SQL Injection Sinks: Injection Sinks — SQL Injection Sinks: Find dynamic ORDER BY, column, or table names derived from input and confirm they are validated against a strict allowlist
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for check dynamic table/column identifiers and order by, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

references.title: OWASP SQL Injection Prevention Cheat Sheet

items.title: Detect NoSQL and ODM injection
items.description: NoSQL databases (MongoDB) are vulnerable when raw query objects, $where, or where() accept attacker-controlled operators.
items.how_to_find: Look for request data passed directly into Mongo query objects, $where/where() clauses, or unparameterized aggregation that allows operator injection.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for detect nosql and odm injection, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

references.title: CWE-943: Improper Neutralization in a Data Query

items.title: Audit stored procedures for dynamic SQL
items.description: Parameterized calls to stored procedures are safe only if the procedure itself does not build dynamic SQL from its parameters.
items.how_to_find: Review stored procedure bodies for EXEC/EXECUTE/sp_executesql on concatenated parameters without QUOTENAME or binding.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for audit stored procedures for dynamic sql, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

references.title: OWASP SQL Injection Prevention Cheat Sheet

sections.title: Injection Sinks — OS Command Injection Sinks

items.title: Flag shell-interpreted command execution
items.description: Calls like system(), os.system, exec with shell=True, Runtime.exec("sh -c ..."), or child_process.exec invoke a shell and are injectable.
items.how_to_find: Injection Sinks — OS Command Injection Sinks: Injection Sinks — OS Command Injection Sinks: Injection Sinks — OS Command Injection Sinks: Injection Sinks — OS Command Injection Sinks: Calls like system(), os.system, exec with shell=True, Runtime.exec("sh -c ..."), or child_process.exec invoke a shell and are injectable
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for flag shell-interpreted command execution, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

references.title: OWASP OS Command Injection Defense Cheat Sheet

items.title: Prefer argument-array execution without a shell
items.description: Even array-based exec is risky if the program/path is attacker-controlled or arguments allow option injection (e.g.
items.how_to_find: Injection Sinks — OS Command Injection Sinks: Injection Sinks — OS Command Injection Sinks: Injection Sinks — OS Command Injection Sinks: Injection Sinks — OS Command Injection Sinks: Confirm command name is fixed/allowlisted, arguments are passed as an array, and option-injection is mitigated with -- separators
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for prefer argument-array execution without a shell, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

references.title: OWASP OS Command Injection Defense Cheat Sheet

items.title: Audit per-language process APIs
items.description: Review Python subprocess/os, Node child_process, Java ProcessBuilder/Runtime, Ruby Kernel#system/backticks, Go os/exec, and PHP exec/passthru/proc_open.
items.how_to_find: Injection Sinks — OS Command Injection Sinks: Injection Sinks — OS Command Injection Sinks: Injection Sinks — OS Command Injection Sinks: Injection Sinks — OS Command Injection Sinks: Enumerate language-specific exec APIs and confirm none receive concatenated untrusted input via a shell
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for audit per-language process apis, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Check indirect command execution via templates and config
items.description: Build scripts, Makefiles, CI YAML, and templating that interpolate input into shell commands are command-injection sinks too.
items.how_to_find: Injection Sinks — OS Command Injection Sinks: Injection Sinks — OS Command Injection Sinks: Injection Sinks — OS Command Injection Sinks: Injection Sinks — OS Command Injection Sinks: Look for run: steps, Makefile recipes, or template-rendered shell commands that embed externally controlled values
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for check indirect command execution via templates and config, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

references.title: OWASP OS Command Injection Defense Cheat Sheet

items.title: Detect file paths built from user input
items.description: Concatenating request data into a file path enables ../ traversal to read or write outside the intended directory.
items.how_to_find: Injection Sinks — Path Traversal Sinks: Injection Sinks — Path Traversal Sinks: Injection Sinks — Path Traversal Sinks: Injection Sinks — Path Traversal Sinks: Find open/read/write/sendFile/include calls where the path includes user input; confirm canonicalization and a base-directory prefix check
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for detect file paths built from user input, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Verify canonicalization happens before the boundary check
items.description: Checks must resolve symlinks and normalize (realpath/Path.normalize) and then verify the result is still within the allowed root.
items.how_to_find: Injection Sinks — Path Traversal Sinks: Injection Sinks — Path Traversal Sinks: Injection Sinks — Path Traversal Sinks: Injection Sinks — Path Traversal Sinks: Confirm the code canonicalizes the path and then validates the resolved path starts with the allowed base, not the raw input
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for verify canonicalization happens before the boundary check, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Account for encoding and null-byte bypasses
items.description: Naive blocklists for ../ are bypassed via URL/double/Unicode encoding or null bytes; rely on canonicalization, not filtering.
items.how_to_find: Injection Sinks — Path Traversal Sinks: Injection Sinks — Path Traversal Sinks: Injection Sinks — Path Traversal Sinks: Injection Sinks — Path Traversal Sinks: Check that traversal defense decodes input fully before validation and is not a simple replace("..", "") blocklist
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for account for encoding and null-byte bypasses, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Check archive extraction for Zip Slip
items.description: Extracting zip/tar entries using their stored names allows ../ entries to write outside the target directory (Zip Slip).
items.how_to_find: Injection Sinks — Path Traversal Sinks: Injection Sinks — Path Traversal Sinks: Injection Sinks — Path Traversal Sinks: Injection Sinks — Path Traversal Sinks: Confirm extraction code validates each entry path resolves inside the destination before writing
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for check archive extraction for zip slip, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Detect Java native deserialization (ObjectInputStream)
items.description: readObject on attacker data with vulnerable gadget classes on the classpath leads to RCE.
items.how_to_find: Injection Sinks — Insecure Deserialization Sinks: Injection Sinks — Insecure Deserialization Sinks: Injection Sinks — Insecure Deserialization Sinks: Injection Sinks — Insecure Deserialization Sinks: Find ObjectInputStream.readObject / XMLDecoder / unsafe Jackson polymorphic typing on untrusted input; confirm look-ahead filters or safe formats
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for detect java native deserialization (objectinputstream), apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Detect Python pickle/yaml.load on untrusted data
items.description: pickle.loads, yaml.load (without SafeLoader), and shelve/jsonpickle on untrusted input execute arbitrary code.
items.how_to_find: Search for pickle.load(s), yaml.load without Loader=SafeLoader, and jsonpickle.decode on data from requests/files/queues.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for detect python pickle/yaml.load on untrusted data, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Detect PHP unserialize() on untrusted input
items.description: unserialize() of attacker data triggers magic methods (__wakeup/__destruct) enabling POP-chain exploitation.
items.how_to_find: Injection Sinks — Insecure Deserialization Sinks: Injection Sinks — Insecure Deserialization Sinks: Injection Sinks — Insecure Deserialization Sinks: Injection Sinks — Insecure Deserialization Sinks: Find unserialize() on cookies, request params, or stored data; confirm allowed_classes is restricted or JSON is used instead
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for detect php unserialize() on untrusted input, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Detect .NET unsafe deserializers
items.description: BinaryFormatter, LosFormatter, NetDataContractSerializer, and TypeNameHandling.All in Json.NET deserialize types unsafely.
items.how_to_find: Search for BinaryFormatter/LosFormatter/SoapFormatter usage and JsonSerializerSettings with TypeNameHandling other than None.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for detect .net unsafe deserializers, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Detect Ruby Marshal.load and YAML.load
items.description: Marshal.load and Psych YAML.load on untrusted data can instantiate arbitrary objects leading to RCE.
items.how_to_find: Find Marshal.load and YAML.load (use YAML.safe_load) on session, cache, or request data.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for detect ruby marshal.load and yaml.load, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

sections.title: Injection Sinks — Template Injection & Dynamic Eval

items.title: Detect server-side template injection (SSTI)

items.title: Detect dynamic code evaluation (eval/exec)
items.description: eval, exec, Function(), setTimeout(string), compile, and runtime code generation on user input execute arbitrary code.

items.title: Check expression-language and SpEL injection

items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for check expression-language and spel injection, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Check dynamic module loading and reflection
items.description: require/import, Class.forName, getattr, and reflection driven by user input can load and invoke unexpected code paths.
items.how_to_find: Injection Sinks — Template Injection & Dynamic Eval: Injection Sinks — Template Injection & Dynamic Eval: Injection Sinks — Template Injection & Dynamic Eval: Injection Sinks — Template Injection & Dynamic Eval: Find dynamic require/import/reflection where the module or class name is user-controlled; confirm an allowlist constrains it

items.title: Detect endpoints missing authentication
items.description: Routes that should require login but lack a guard/middleware/decorator are unauthenticated access points.
items.how_to_find: Map each protected route to its auth middleware/decorator; flag handlers reachable without authentication that touch sensitive data.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for detect endpoints missing authentication, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

references.title: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

items.title: Verify password/token comparison is constant-time
items.description: Comparing secrets with == leaks length/timing; use constant-time comparison, and verify passwords via slow KDFs.
items.how_to_find: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Authentication Logic: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Authentication Logic: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Authentication Logic: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Authentication Logic: Find secret comparisons using == or .equals; confirm constant-time helpers (hmac.compare_digest, crypto.timingSafeEqual) for tokens
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for verify password/token comparison is constant-time, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Verify password hashing uses a strong KDF
items.description: Passwords must be stored with bcrypt, scrypt, Argon2, or PBKDF2 with appropriate cost — never plaintext or fast hashes.
items.how_to_find: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Authentication Logic: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Authentication Logic: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Authentication Logic: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Authentication Logic: Find password persistence; confirm a slow salted KDF is used with sane parameters and no fast hash (MD5/SHA-x) or plaintext
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for verify password hashing uses a strong kdf, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

references.title: CWE-916: Use of Password Hash With Insufficient Computational Effort

items.title: Check brute-force protection on auth endpoints
items.description: Login, OTP, and password-reset endpoints need rate limiting and lockout to resist credential stuffing and brute force.
items.how_to_find: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Authentication Logic: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Authentication Logic: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Authentication Logic: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Authentication Logic: Confirm authentication and reset flows enforce rate limiting/lockout and that error messages do not enable user enumeration
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for check brute-force protection on auth endpoints, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

references.title: CWE-307: Improper Restriction of Excessive Authentication Attempts

items.title: Look for authentication logic bypasses
items.description: Flawed boolean logic, early returns, default-allow branches, or unverified "remember me" flows can bypass authentication.
items.how_to_find: Trace auth decision logic for default-allow paths, inverted conditions, or states where verification is skipped.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for look for authentication logic bypasses, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Detect missing object-level authorization (IDOR)
items.description: Handlers that fetch a resource by id from input without verifying ownership allow access to other users records.
items.how_to_find: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Access Control: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Access Control: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Access Control: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Access Control: For each by-id fetch, confirm an ownership/tenant check ties the resource to the authenticated principal before returning or modifying it
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for detect missing object-level authorization (idor), apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

references.title: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

items.title: Detect missing function-level authorization
items.description: Admin/privileged actions must check role/permission server-side, not rely on hidden UI or client-side gating.
items.how_to_find: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Access Control: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Access Control: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Access Control: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Access Control: Confirm privileged handlers verify role/permission server-side; flag any that depend solely on UI hiding or client checks
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for detect missing function-level authorization, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Check for mass assignment / over-posting
items.description: Binding request bodies directly to models lets attackers set protected fields (isAdmin, role, balance).
items.how_to_find: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Access Control: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Access Control: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Access Control: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Access Control: Find model binding from request bodies; confirm an allowlist of writable fields or DTOs excludes privilege/ownership fields
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for check for mass assignment / over-posting, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

references.title: CWE-915: Improperly Controlled Modification of Object Attributes

items.title: Verify tenant isolation in multi-tenant queries
items.description: Every data query in a multi-tenant system must be scoped by tenant id to prevent cross-tenant data exposure.
items.how_to_find: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Access Control: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Access Control: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Access Control: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Access Control: Confirm queries include a tenant/org filter derived from the session, not from request input, and that no global queries leak across tenants

items.title: Confirm authorization is centralized and deny-by-default
items.description: Scattered, ad-hoc permission checks are error-prone; a central policy layer that defaults to deny is more reliable.
items.how_to_find: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Access Control: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Access Control: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Access Control: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Access Control: Scattered, ad-hoc permission checks are error-prone; a central policy layer that defaults to deny is more reliable
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for confirm authorization is centralized and deny-by-default, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

sections.title: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Session & Token Handling

items.title: Verify session cookie security attributes
items.description: Session cookies must set HttpOnly, Secure, and an appropriate SameSite to resist theft and CSRF.

items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for verify session cookie security attributes, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Check session regeneration on privilege change
items.description: Sessions must be regenerated on login and privilege elevation to prevent session fixation.

items.title: Verify JWT signature and algorithm validation
items.description: JWT verification must enforce a fixed algorithm and reject alg=none; never trust unsigned or attacker-chosen-algorithm tokens.

items.title: Check token expiry, revocation, and refresh handling
items.description: Tokens need bounded lifetimes, server-side revocation for stateful needs (logout, compromise), and safe refresh rotation.
items.how_to_find: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Session & Token Handling: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Session & Token Handling: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Session & Token Handling: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Session & Token Handling: Confirm tokens carry expiry, are checked, and that logout/revocation actually invalidates them; review refresh-token rotation and reuse detection

references.title: OWASP JSON Web Token Cheat Sheet

items.title: Verify session/token identifiers are unpredictable
items.description: Session ids and reset tokens must come from a CSPRNG with sufficient entropy, not sequential ids or weak RNG.
items.how_to_find: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Session & Token Handling: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Session & Token Handling: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Session & Token Handling: AuthN & AuthZ Flaws — Session & Token Handling: Find token generation; confirm a cryptographically secure RNG and adequate length, not Math.random/rand or predictable sequences

references.title: CWE-330: Use of Insufficiently Random Values

items.title: Detect secrets committed in source code
items.description: API keys, passwords, private keys, and tokens hardcoded in code are exposed to anyone with repo access.
items.how_to_find: Scan for high-entropy strings and known key formats (AKIA, -----BEGIN PRIVATE KEY-----, slack/stripe tokens) in tracked files.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for detect secrets committed in source code, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Check config files and committed env files for secrets
items.description: Secrets in application.properties, appsettings.json, .env, or Helm/Terraform files frequently get committed by mistake.

items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for check config files and committed env files for secrets, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

references.title: CWE-256: Plaintext Storage of a Password

items.title: Scan git history for previously committed secrets
items.description: Removing a secret from HEAD does not remove it from history; leaked secrets must be rotated, not just deleted.
items.how_to_find: Cryptography & Secrets — Hardcoded Secrets: Cryptography & Secrets — Hardcoded Secrets: Cryptography & Secrets — Hardcoded Secrets: Cryptography & Secrets — Hardcoded Secrets: Run a history-aware scanner across all commits; confirm any historical secret has been rotated/revoked, not merely deleted from the latest commit
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for scan git history for previously committed secrets, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Check client bundles and mobile apps for embedded secrets
items.description: Secrets shipped in frontend JS bundles or mobile binaries are public; only public/anon keys belong client-side.
items.how_to_find: Search built JS bundles, source maps, and decompiled mobile apps for API keys or backend credentials that should be server-only.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for check client bundles and mobile apps for embedded secrets, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Detect weak hash algorithms (MD5/SHA1)
items.description: MD5 and SHA1 are broken for integrity/signatures and unsuitable for passwords; flag their security use.
items.how_to_find: Find MD5/SHA1 usage; determine whether it is security-relevant (signatures, integrity, password) and recommend SHA-256+ or a KDF.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for detect weak hash algorithms (md5/sha1), apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

references.title: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

items.title: Detect insecure cipher modes (ECB) and missing auth
items.description: ECB leaks patterns and unauthenticated CBC is malleable; prefer authenticated modes like AES-GCM.

items.title: Detect static/reused IVs and nonces

items.title: Detect weak random number generation for secrets
